our_argument_summary: EXP-253 wholesale-rewrites §4.2.2 to lead with the dome's published misstatement of standard-physics eclipse prediction (Domain 11 falsification rule + countdown banner both treat 0 nT as the 'globe prediction'), preserves the existing Chapman 1933 paragraph as supporting prose, and closes with a 'BOU 2017 already falsifies by the dome's own ±3 nT rule' hammer paragraph linking to §4.2.5. A secondary one-sentence prepend on §4.2.3 names the dependency on §4.2.2's misstatement so the dual-baseline hedging argument reads as a structural consequence rather than a self-contained critique.

quote_verification_independent.drift_status: no drift detected since analyst's 2026-04-26 verification

universality_of_zero_framing.question: Is 'globe predicts 0' framing universal across dome eclipse material, or limited to live-power Domain 11 + countdown banner?
universality_of_zero_framing.finding: The framing appears in EXACTLY two places: (a) live-power Domain 11 falsification criterion, (b) eclipse countdown banner JS template. Outside live.html, the dome's predictions registry (raw-text/03-predictions.txt) gives only ECM predicted values (E001-E009 station-by-station, BOU baseline -5 to -12.8 nT, W004 baseline -10.3 to -26.2 nT) and never assigns a numerical value to 'globe predicts X' at all. The dome's home page (raw-text/01-home.txt) and wins/model pages also do not.
universality_of_zero_framing.implication: The lead-paragraph claim 'The dome treats zero, not five-to-twenty-five, as the standard-physics expectation' is supported only by the falsification rule and the countdown banner — i.e., by the two artifacts where the dome explicitly assigns a value to 'globe prediction.' These ARE the dome's only published statements of what the globe predicts, so the framing is defensible — but the body language could be tightened to match the TLDR, which appropriately narrows scope to 'the dome's published falsification criterion.'
universality_of_zero_framing.severity: minor — phrasing precision, not a factual hole

bou_already_falsifies_overreach_test.question: Could a dome defender argue BOU 2017 was a 'globe-era' measurement and the ±3 nT rule applies only to 2026, escaping the trilemma?
bou_already_falsifies_overreach_test.defender_strongest_response: The Domain 11 falsification rule's domain of applicability was registered for the 2026 measurement; BOU 2017 is an INPUT to the 2026 prediction formula, not the measurement under test. The rule does not apply retroactively to the baseline.
bou_already_falsifies_overreach_test.counter_to_defender: If the rule's domain of applicability is 2026-only, then we can ask: what does the rule say globe predicts for the 2026 measurement? The dome's own formula (delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF) yields predicted values of ~-5 to -26 nT at the prediction stations — i.e., the dome's prediction is itself baseline-anchored to BOU 2017's globe-physics measurement. So either (i) globe-physics-2026 also predicts ~-10 nT (consistent with the dome), in which case the test is non-discriminating, or (ii) globe-physics-2026 predicts 0 (per the rule), in which case the dome is using a globe-physics measurement to predict its own discrimination test — a contradiction. The trilemma in EXP-253's closing paragraph captures this collapse correctly: accept rule → globe is wrong; revise rule → non-discriminating.
bou_already_falsifies_overreach_test.verdict: trilemma is robust; defender's strongest response collapses into the second horn (revise rule → non-discriminating) which the closing paragraph names. No over-reach.

independent_bou_2017_minus_10_9_check.question: Is the −10.9 nT BOU 2017 figure correct?
sources_consulted: EXP-253 cites existing §4.2.5 prose and WIN-010 detail_verdict_text (post-EXP-228/EXP-244 framing)
raw-text/02-wins.txt line 81 (dome's own WIN-010 source claim): 'INTERMAGNET, Boulder Observatory. -10.9 nT anomaly at 17:20 UTC coinciding with eclipse maximum.'
raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 23: 'Baselines: BOU 2017 (-10.9 nT) | W004 empirical (-22.24 nT)' — dome's published baseline
raw-text/05-model.txt line 73: 'coupling = 10.9 nT / 6.5 uGal = 1.67 nT/uGal' — dome's coupling derivation
raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 226: 'Source: BOU 2017 eclipse' — dome's coupling source citation
independent_bou_2017_minus_10_9_check.independent_primary_source: NOT independently verified against a USGS/INTERMAGNET primary data file in this review. The figure is the dome's own canonical baseline, used consistently across the dome's WIN list, predictions registry, model description, and live-power dashboard. EXP-253's closing arithmetic (|−10.9| ≈ 3.6× the ±3 nT half-width) is internally consistent with that figure.
independent_bou_2017_minus_10_9_check.verdict: −10.9 nT is the dome's published canonical baseline and is internally consistent across dome artifacts. Independent NOAA/USGS verification remains a deferred recommendation (already noted as out-of-scope in EXP-253). If the dome silently revises the baseline (it has refined parameters periodically), the closing paragraph's '3.6×' arithmetic must be re-derived. Inheriting the figure from existing review framing is acceptable for this EXP.

proposal_review.structural_holes_introduced: none — the new prose preserves the load-bearing Chapman paragraph and 'no physical mechanism' rebuttal verbatim, adds two leading paragraphs (misstatement framing + verbatim quotes), and adds one closing paragraph (BOU-already-falsifies hammer). The cross-link forward to §4.2.3 (already exists at id=p4-423) and §4.2.5 (already exists at id=p4-425) both resolve. The cross-link to §4.2.7 in EXP-253's metadata is NOT in the prose patch (correctly deferred) — §4.2.7 (id=p4-427) does not yet exist in sections.json.
proposal_review.secondary_patch_review: The §4.2.3 prepend reads as one long compound sentence: 'The dual-baseline design only reads as a discrimination test because (per §4.2.2) the dome misstates the globe prediction as zero; with the actual Chapman range (5–25 nT) restored, the dome's window overlaps the globe's expectation rather than excluding it.' The semicolon splice carries two distinct claims (the dependency, and the consequence). Splitting into two sentences would aid readability without changing meaning. Substring uniqueness verified: the 'before' string occurs exactly once in part4.html. Patch is applicable as-is; suggest copy-edit only.

holes_found.description: Closing paragraph wording '3.6× outside the ±3 nT envelope' is non-standard and ambiguous. Reading 1: 'the magnitude is 3.6× the envelope half-width' (correct: 10.9 ≈ 3.6 × 3). Reading 2: 'the value sits 3.6 envelope-widths past the edge' (different, would be (10.9 - 3) / 3 = 2.6×). The intended meaning is reading 1, but the prose phrasing 'outside the envelope' nudges readers toward reading 2. A defender would seize on this to argue the arithmetic claim is loose.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace with explicit-arithmetic phrasing. Suggested: 'the BOU 2017 baseline of −10.9 nT is roughly 3.6× the falsification half-width (|−10.9| / 3 ≈ 3.6), well outside the dome's own ±3 nT acceptance window.' OR keep the parenthetical but reframe to: 'one of its own baseline inputs sits at −10.9 nT, more than 3× the rule's ±3 nT half-width past zero.'

holes_found.description: Lead paragraph uses 'replaced with a strawman' to describe the dome's misrepresentation: 'any non-zero measurement at the prediction stations becomes confirmation of ECM, falsification of globe — but only because the globe's actual prediction has been replaced with a strawman.' The verb 'replaced' implies deliberate substitution, which strays from 'the claim is wrong' and toward 'the claim was knowingly misrepresented.' EXP-253's anticipated_objections #4 defends 'wrong' over 'misspoke' but does not engage the stronger 'strawman' connotation. A defender could argue this characterization is uncharitable — that 'globe predicts 0' might just be a shorthand the dome author has not internalized as a Chapman-disagreement.
holes_found.recommendation: Soften to a structural rather than intent-laden verb. Suggested: 'misrepresented as zero,' 'displaced by a zero-baseline,' or simply 'the globe's actual 5–25 nT prediction is treated as zero in the dome's published rule.' Preserves the critique without imputing motive.

holes_found.description: Body sentence 'The dome treats zero, not five-to-twenty-five, as the standard-physics expectation' implies universal dome stance. The TLDR appropriately scopes this to 'the dome's published falsification criterion treats 0 nT as the test'; the body should match. The framing is supported only by Domain 11 + countdown banner — the predictions registry never makes a 'globe predicts X' claim.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten to 'The dome's published falsification criterion and live countdown banner treat zero, not five-to-twenty-five, as the standard-physics expectation.' Two-word addition; preserves rhetorical punch while matching the TLDR scope.

holes_found.description: Secondary §4.2.3 prepend is one long compound sentence with a semicolon splice. The two claims (dependency on §4.2.2 + consequence: window overlaps Chapman range) are distinct enough to split.
holes_found.recommendation: Split into: 'The dual-baseline design only reads as a discrimination test because (per §4.2.2) the dome misstates the globe prediction as zero. With the actual Chapman range (5–25 nT) restored, the dome's window overlaps the globe's expectation rather than excluding it.' Same content, two sentences.

holes_found.description: TLDR is ~85 words across three sentences (analyst's anticipated_objection #5 acknowledges this). CLAUDE.md Progressive Disclosure spec calls for '2–3 sentence TLDRs' — three sentences is at the upper bound, and each carries weight, but a reader scanning the section list sees a denser block than other §4.2.x TLDRs. Defensible but on the long end.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: trim middle clause. Current second sentence: 'The dome's published falsification criterion treats globe predicts 0 nT as the test; standard ionospheric physics (Chapman 1933) actually predicts 5–25 nT, confirmed across dozens of eclipses over nearly a century, and one of the dome's own baseline inputs already sits outside its ±3 nT globe window.' Could cut '...confirmed across dozens of eclipses over nearly a century, and...' to leave the BOU-already-falsifies hammer in the third sentence as the standalone punchline. Net: ~70 words, three short sentences. Not blocking.

holes_found.description: Forward-link to §4.2.7 is in EXP-253's cross_references metadata but NOT in the prose patches — and §4.2.7 (id=p4-427) does not yet exist in sections.json. This is correctly deferred per HNOTE AMENDMENT-001 ordering. No actionable hole; flagged for completeness — when §4.2.7 lands (EXP-254), §4.2.2 should be re-edited to add the cross-link.
holes_found.recommendation: After EXP-254 (§4.2.7 creation) integrates, file a follow-up micro-patch to insert the forward-link in §4.2.2. No change needed in this EXP.

stronger_arguments.description: Add an inline arithmetic anchor that names the dome's own predicted 2026 values. The dome's published predictions (E001-E009 in raw-text/03-predictions.txt) are -5.0 to -12.8 nT (BOU baseline) and -10.3 to -26.2 nT (W004 baseline). Both ranges sit ENTIRELY outside the dome's ±3 nT 'globe prediction' window. So even if a defender insists the rule applies to the 2026 measurement only (not retroactively to BOU 2017), the dome's own 2026 prediction would BY ITSELF satisfy the falsification criterion against globe = 0 — meaning the rule doesn't discriminate dome from globe in advance, it just requires that the measurement not be near zero. This is a tighter version of the second-horn collapse than the EXP currently states.
sources: raw-text/03-predictions.txt lines 22-30 (dome's published E001-E009 station predictions)

stronger_arguments.description: The 'no physical mechanism' rebuttal paragraph (preserved from prior version) has a quote-source gap. Where does the dome's site say the globe has 'no physical mechanism' for eclipse magnetic effects? Verifying or removing the embedded quote would harden the argument. Outside the scope of EXP-253 but worth filing as a follow-up.
sources: raw-text/ search needed for 'no physical mechanism' or 'no mechanism'

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-253's kernel-of-truth field is well-constructed. It correctly preserves two genuine dome contributions: (1) the eclipse magnetic anomaly is a real, measurable phenomenon (not fabricated); (2) the dome's prediction does include a physical-mechanism placeholder — delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF — with FSF ratio derivation across nine stations at Pearson r ≈ 0.9999 (per WIN-010 post-EXP-220/EXP-228 concessions).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The new lead does not deny the anomaly, the formula structure, or the FSF ratio derivation. It denies only that the dome's published 'globe prediction = 0' represents standard physics. That denial is supported by the dome's own falsification rule juxtaposed with its own input baseline. The kernel-of-truth concessions remain intact under the new prose.

deeper_analysis: This EXP makes explicit a critique that has been implicitly load-bearing across §4.2.3 / §4.2.5 / WIN-010 detail prose for several prior expansions: the dome's 'globe predicts 0' framing is what makes the eclipse prediction look discriminating in the first place. Lifting that critique into the lead of §4.2.2 reorders the section's rhetorical structure to put the misstatement front and center, before Chapman is introduced — meaning a reader who clicks through to §4.2.2 understands within the first 100 words why the dome's discrimination claim is built on a strawman. The closing trilemma (accept rule → globe is wrong; revise rule → non-discriminating) is rhetorically sharper than the prior version's implicit framing and survives the strongest defender responses tested in this review (rule-domain-applicability and shorthand-not-literal-claim defenses both collapse into the second horn). The verbatim-quote strategy (falsification rule + countdown banner) is fragile to dome-edits, but the in-repo archived form (raw-text/08-live-power.txt) plus curmudgeon-time re-verification (this run) plus the live_quote_verification block in the EXP provide layered defenses. My quote re-verification at this run confirms both quotes are still byte-current on live.html, matching exactly what the analyst captured. The remaining concerns are all phrasing-level (3.6× idiom, strawman vs displaced, body-vs-TLDR scope mismatch, semicolon splice in §4.2.3 prepend, TLDR length); none threaten the argument structure. Approve with minor copy-edits.

summary_for_decider: APPROVE WITH MINOR EDITS. The misstatement-lead rewrite of §4.2.2 is sound: both verbatim dome quotes are confirmed current on live.html as of 2026-04-26 (exact byte match to EXP claims), the BOU-already-falsifies trilemma survives the strongest defender responses I could construct, and the kernel-of-truth concessions are preserved. Four minor copy-edits recommended: (1) replace '3.6× outside the ±3 nT envelope' with explicit-arithmetic phrasing to remove ambiguity (moderate severity — readability hole a defender could exploit); (2) soften 'replaced with a strawman' to 'misrepresented as zero' or similar (moderate — avoids imputing intent); (3) tighten body sentence 'The dome treats zero...' to match TLDR's narrower scope ('published falsification criterion and live countdown banner treat zero...') (minor); (4) split the §4.2.3 prepended semicolon-spliced sentence into two for readability (minor). Optional: trim TLDR by ~15 words. No structural rewrite needed; no verdict-equivalent change needed. Patches are substring-unique and applicable to current sections.json. Not batchable per Step 8a (severity is moderate per ISS-1550).
